To this end, it chronicled the experiences of the victims of 
Section 377, relying on the extensive records and affidavits 
submitted by the Petitioners that brought to fore instances 
of custodial rape and torture, social boycott, degrading and 
inhuman treatment and incarceration. The court concluded 
that while Section 377 criminalized conduct, it created a 
systemic pattern of disadvantage, exclusion and indignity 
for the LGBT community, and for individuals who indulge 
in non-heterosexual conduct.
